mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through reduced aggregate demand and profit margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:September 2009 payroll employment at 131.2M, down from 2008 peak of 138M
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with persistent labor slack
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS momentum and 20d forward returns exceeds 0.05 in absolute value, or negative PAYEMS changes are followed by positive SPX returns in > 55% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals an exhausted monetary policy transmission mechanism and market saturation with liquidity, removing the tailwind from rate cuts and producing negative forward returns as investors reprrice risk premia upward.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for 3+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% in August 2009, near zero lower bound
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in > 65% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward returns is positive and exceeds 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with expanding housing starts (HOUST) signals strengthening consumer demand and construction activity that supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 351.4B and HOUST at 581K units in July 2009, both recovering from 2008 lows
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST is followed by negative 20d SPX returns in > 50% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines signal contraction in manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side weakness that constrains profit margins and future growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices near-term earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 95.97 in July 2009, still 30% below 2007 peak
regime_hint: post-shock supply-side recovery with persistent slack
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO declines > 0.5% are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO changes and 5d forward returns is positive and exceeds 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with stagnant payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals persistent labor market slack and weak wage growth that reduces consumer purchasing power and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through demand destruction.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 9.5% and PAYEMS shows no growth for 2+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.7% and PAYEMS flat in August 2009
failure_condition: Instances meeting the trigger condition are followed by positive 60d SPX returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction coefficient between UNRATE and PAYEMS momentum is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI (CPIAUCSL) declining or flat signals deflationary pressure and reduced nominal growth expectations that compress equity valuations through lower nominal earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change < 0.1% for 2+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 214.47 in July 2009, showing subdued inflation
failure_condition: Periods of subdued CPI growth (< 0.1% MoM) are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in > 65% of instances, or Rank IC between CPI momentum and forward returns exceeds 0.04 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerating above 600K annualized units signals strengthening residential construction demand and wealth effects from housing recovery that support equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through multiplier effects on employment and consumption.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above 600K units and maintains level for 2+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 581K in July 2009, approaching recovery threshold
regime_hint: housing market stabilization and recovery phase
failure_condition: HOUST sustained above 600K is followed by negative 60d SPX returns in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC between HOUST level and forward returns is negative and exceeds -0.03 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust aggregate demand expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both demand components to be expanding simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 12892.5B in Q2 2009 showing early recovery, RSAFS at 351.4B in August 2009
failure_condition: Simultaneous positive momentum in GDPC1 and RSAFS is followed by negative 20d SPX returns in > 50% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 2-year rolling window.
